name: Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop
name_original: Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal — circular por la Sierra de Albarracín desde Jabaloyas
description: This moderate 18.2-mile mountain bike loop from Jabaloyas explores Monte Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal in Sierra de Albarracín.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Jabaloyas offers a diverse landscape characterized by the Montes Universales mountain range, river valleys, and forested areas. The region features varied terrain, from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents, making it suitable for different skill levels. Riders can expect to encounter natural features such as waterfalls and elevated viewpoints. The trails often wind through pine forests and open areas, providing a mix of technical sections and scenic routes. Best mountain bike trails around Jabaloyas The most popular mountain bike trail is Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop, a 16.3 miles (26.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores river valleys and natural waterfalls. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe, a moderate 19.3 miles (31.0 km) path. This route traverses the Montes Universales, offering expansive views and forested sections. Local mountain bikers also love the Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop, a 18.2 miles (29.4 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Mountain biking around Jabaloyas is defined by varied elevations, dense pine forests, and open mountain landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more advanced mountain biking challenges. The routes in Jabaloyas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 14 reviews. More than 150 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Jabaloyas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Jabaloyas?
A: While there are no trails specifically graded as 'easy' in the Jabaloyas area, many of the 14 moderate routes offer a good introduction to mountain biking for those with some experience. These trails often feature varied terrain and manageable climbs, providing a rewarding experience without extreme technical demands.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Jabaloyas?
A: The mountain bike trails in Jabaloyas traverse a diverse landscape, including the Montes Universales mountain range, river valleys, and dense pine forests. Riders will encounter varied elevations, from moderate climbs to more challenging ascents, with a mix of technical sections and scenic routes through open mountain landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls in Jabaloyas?
A: Yes, the region is known for its natural waterfalls. You can explore routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which specifically highlights these natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247809" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herrería Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/604533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Mill Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable attractions along some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Jabaloyas?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Jabaloyas, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow in higher parts of the Montes Universales, making some trails more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are there any long-distance or advanced mountain bike routes in Jabaloyas?
A: Yes, Jabaloyas offers several challenging routes for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabrerizo Viewpoint – Albarracín loop from Dornaque&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 71.4 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557077" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area with Barbecues – Jabaloyas loop from Jabaloyas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 48 km.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Jabaloyas?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Jabaloyas are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003616464/pico-javalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Jabaloyas?
A: The mountain bike routes in Jabaloyas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 14 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse terrain, the scenic beauty of the Montes Universales, and the rewarding experience of exploring river valleys and forested sections.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003616464/pico-javalon-and-ermita-de-san-cristobal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalón and Ermita de San Cristóbal Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical sites. Additionally, natural attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/756481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Bezas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel&lt;/a&gt; are accessible from various routes.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Jabaloyas typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Jabaloyas varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003543651/ojos-del-cabriel-herreria-and-san-pedro-waterfalls-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos del Cabriel, Herrería and San Pedro Waterfalls Loop&lt;/a&gt; (26.2 km) can take around 2 hours 34 minutes, while longer, more difficult routes can extend to 6 hours or more.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that start from specific trailheads or easily accessible points?
A: Many routes are designed to be accessible from common starting points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008304509/montes-universales-vuelta-desde-el-camping-algarbe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes Universales — vuelta desde el Camping Algarbe&lt;/a&gt; explicitly starts from Camping Algarbe, providing a clear trailhead. Other routes often begin from villages or designated parking areas within the region.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in Jabaloyas suitable for families with children?
A: Given that most trails are graded as moderate or difficult, they are generally better suited for families with older children who have prior mountain biking experience. The varied terrain and elevation changes require a certain level of fitness and skill. There are no 'easy' trails specifically designed for very young or novice family riders.
tips: We are in Jabaloyas, a town located in the Sierra de Albarracín and at the foot of Mount Javalón. In the province of Teruel it is known as the town of witches and offers us beautiful landscapes of pine trees, limestone rocks and water sources that make this environment a special place for lovers of two wheels.
Beautiful views from this mountain, which is full of legends about witches.
It has barbecues that can be used as shelter

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for mountain biking the Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal circular route is approximately 2 hours and 40 minutes. This is an average time and can vary based on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal route is a circular trail, starting and ending in Jabaloyas.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The route features mixed terrain, including forest roads and some singletrack sections. You'll ride through beautiful pine forests and enjoy spectacular views of the surrounding Sierra de Albarracín landscape.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass through the charming settlement of Jabaloyas and ascend to Pico Javalón, which offers unbeatable views. There's also a Picnic Area with Barbecues available for a stop.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While some sections in the broader Jabaloyas area might be suitable for families or beginners, this specific trail generally requires a good fitness level and advanced riding skills for mountain biking. Riders should be prepared for varying terrain and elevation changes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Albarracín is beautiful throughout the year, but spring is particularly recommended if you wish to see water features at their fullest after snowmelt. Generally, late spring, summer, and early autumn offer pleasant conditions for mountain biking.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Jabaloyas?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Jabaloyas, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but local signage or inquiries upon arrival should guide you.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 24% of this route overlaps with sections of the official trail known as Rubiales - Orihuela del Tremedal.

faqs.question: Is the Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Albarracín generally welcomes dogs on trails, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wild fauna in this unspoiled landscape. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Monte Javalón y Ermita de San Cristóbal trail. The Sierra de Albarracín is a natural area open for public enjoyment, but always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Albarracín is known for its practically unspoiled landscapes, making it relatively easy to encounter various wild fauna along the routes. Keep an eye out for local birds, mammals, and other wildlife native to the region.
